methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Base Power is a Series C climate and energy company founded by Zach Dell and Justin Lopas that has raised over $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lenza: LNZA) across the Climate & Energy sector, including peers such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, Amogy, Helion, and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $1.7B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-stage, same-sector comparables: KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M. The median valuation across this peer set is $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B.

The two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $850M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.4B. This range reflects the inherent tension between peer-derived floor and the step-up methodology's upside case. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-company peer set given the broader population of nine private comparables, and the applicability of stage-progression multiples to Base Power's specific technology and market trajectory within frontier energy infrastructure.
